title: Private key PEM not zeroized after use in signing paths
description: Private key PEM strings are fetched from the database as plain Strings (persona.rs:129-137 get_private_key returns String) and passed through signing functions (delivery.rs:214, server.rs:834). After use, these Strings are dropped normally without zeroization, leaving key material in freed heap memory until overwritten. The rsa crate's Zeroizing<String> from initial generation (persona.rs:20-22) is correctly noted, but the .to_string() copy and all subsequent DB reads bypass zeroization. The fix is to wrap the returned private key in zeroize::Zeroizing<String> from get_private_key through to the signing callsites. This is a confidentiality issue: key material persists in process memory. Locations: persona.rs:129-137 (get_private_key returns plain String), delivery.rs:214 (private_key as String on stack), server.rs:834 (private_key as String on stack), signatures.rs:16-24 (sign_request takes &str, parses to RsaPrivateKey which the rsa crate does zeroize on drop, but the PEM &str source is not).
close_reason: rsa crate's RsaPrivateKey doesn't implement Zeroize — parsed key material persists regardless. Wrapping PEM string alone provides minimal benefit.
